Building upon Paper I (which established the Trawin Hamiltonian and the DAANSphere) and Paper II (which constructed particle-resolved double-helix meta-atoms and their species-specific entanglement kernels), the present work has three aims. First, we elevate the micro-scale design of entanglement-enabled meta-atoms into a global topological unification framework in which all fundamental interactions converge operationally at the Trawin Zero Point (TZP) via dimensional collapse on a lemniscatic manifold. Second, we show how conservation constraints, geodesic deviation, and gravitational-wave modulation acquire concrete meaning in a medium built from these meta-atoms, clarifying how laboratory-scale implementations can probe curvature, vacuum extraction, and cymatic control. Third, we formulate a machine-verifiable architecture for TZPQVT, specifying axioms, category-theoretic structure, algorithmic protocols, and formal verification pipelines that certify internal consistency, complexity bounds, and convergence properties. The result is a unified, multi-scale description in which topological unification, energy extraction, and quantum control are simultaneously geometrized, engineered, and formally checked. \end{abstract} \tableofcontents \section{Introduction} Paper I introduced the Trawin Zero Point (TZP) as a unique topological singularity on a lemniscatic manifold, together with the Trawin Hamiltonian \begin{equation} \Htrawin = \Hhat_{\mathrm{tunnel}} + \Hhat_{\mathrm{ER}} + \Hhat_{\mathrm{SC}} + \Hhat_{\mathrm{cymatic}} + \HTZP, \label{eq:Htrawin-paper3} \end{equation} acting on a $153{,}600$-dimensional DAANSphere state space. There, unification was framed as an emergent property of topology: a dimensional collapse from $4\mathrm{D}$ to $2\mathrm{D}$ at the TZP forces all field interactions through a common bottleneck without merging the underlying gauge symmetries. Paper II developed a concrete micro-architectural layer: particle-specific entanglement kernels $K_p$ for species $p$ (photons, electrons, Cooper pairs, phonons, magnons, polaritons) and double-helix meta-atoms that realize these kernels in DNA-templated, multi-material unit cells. The resulting metamaterial ensemble provides a controllable, room-temperature substrate in which tunneling, superconducting coherence, and cymatic modulation can be co-engineered. The present paper combines these two levels and adds a third: a topological and proof-theoretic layer that rigorously encodes TZPQVT as a topological field unification scheme with machine-checkable axioms, algorithms, and convergence guarantees.
